DISCOVERY 612, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF PAUL PERDUE'S MOTION FOR ORDER IMPOSING ISSUE SANCTIONS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED AS IT SEEKS ISSUE SANCTIONS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE LATE COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT ORDER TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT $412.50, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-06-2008 Off Calendar

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-18-2008 Set For Case Management Conference On Mar-21-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Issue Sanction And Awarding Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Jan-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Paul Perdue's Motion For Order Imposing Issue Sanctions And For Monetary Sanctions is denied as it seeks issue sanctions. The motion is granted as it seeks monetary sanctions. The late compliance with the court order to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount $412.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn For Order Compelling Appearance At Deposition And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Dec-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Paul L. Perdue's Motion To Compel Appearance At Deposition is ordered to appear for her deposition in compliance with the stipulation. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To First Set Of Interrogatories And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Constantin V. Roboostoff, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Paul R. Perdue
James M. Millar, Eas., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Tina Tan

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Paul R. Perdue's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Interrogatories is granted. Defendant is ordered to respond within 30 days of notice of this order. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $865, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this motion. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Oct-17-2007 Denied

DEFENDANT.
NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

RULING - ATTY. RICHARD NELSON OF MILLAR & ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS DEFENDANT Tina Tan' ATTORNEY OF RECORD IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF RENEWING MOTION. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =(301/PJB)
